revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん、前の山古墳とも）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里3丁目にある[[前方後円墳]...'
revision.text:
'''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん、前の山古墳とも）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里3丁目にある[[前方後円墳]]。現在、宮内庁によって、[[日本武尊]]（[[景行天皇]]皇子）白鳥陵に治定されている。
==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、白髪山古墳の北東に位置する。全長約190ｍ、後円部径約106ｍ、高さ約20ｍ、前方部幅約165ｍ、高さ約23ｍで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で、くびれ部の両側には造り出しがある。周囲には幅約35ｍの周濠と上面幅21ｍの堤がめぐらされている。
主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の円筒[[埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの形象埴輪が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
[[Category:古墳|かるさとおおつかこふん]] [[Category:羽曳野市|かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub|かるさとおおつかこふん}}

{{日本の古墳| 名称=軽里大塚古墳| 所在地=大阪府羽曳野市軽里3丁目| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|5|N|135|36|14|E|scale:20000}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀後半| 規模=全長190m、高さ23m| 史跡指定=| 被葬者=[[日本武尊]]（[[宮内庁]]治定）| 出土品=埴輪| 特記事項=宮内庁治定（白鳥陵）| 画像=[[画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun.jpg|none|250px]] }} [[画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{国土航空写真}}]]
'''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん、'''前の山古墳'''とも）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里3丁目にある[[前方後円墳]]。現在、[[宮内庁]]によって、[[日本武尊]]（[[景行天皇]]皇子）白鳥陵に治定されており、羽曳野市の名前の由来ともなっている。
==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、[[白髪山古墳]]の北東に位置する。全長約190m、後円部径約106m、高さ約20m、前方部幅約165m、高さ約23mで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で前方部を西に向け、くびれ部の両側には造り出しがある。周囲には幅約35mの周濠と上面幅21mの堤がめぐらされている。
主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の円筒[[埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの形象埴輪が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。
== アクセス == [[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]より西に徒歩約10分
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かるさとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史‎]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|陵かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の古墳| 名称=軽里大塚古墳
（前の山古墳）| 所属=[[古市古墳群]]| 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里3丁目| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=軽里大塚古墳（伝日本武尊陵）}}| 形状=[[前方後円墳]]| 築造年代=[[5世紀]]後半| 規模=墳丘長190m
高さ23m| 史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定「白鳥陵」| 被葬者=[[日本武尊]]（[[宮内庁]]治定）| 出土品=[[埴輪]]| 特記事項=全国第35位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2015年8月16日更新版）。| 画像=[[画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun.jpg|none|250px]] }} [[画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{国土航空写真}}]] '''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん、）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 [[宮内庁]]により「'''白鳥陵'''（しらとりのみささぎ）」として第12代[[景行天皇]]皇子の[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の陵に治定されている。 別称を「'''前の山古墳'''」とも。羽曳野市の名前の由来となった古墳である。 ==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、[[白髪山古墳]]の北東に位置する。全長約190m、後円部径約106m、高さ約20m、前方部幅約165m、高さ約23mで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で前方部を西に向け、くびれ部の両側には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲には幅約35mの周濠と上面幅21mの堤がめぐらされている。 主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の円筒[[埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの形象埴輪が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。 == アクセス == [[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]より西に徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるさとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史‎]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: 陵墓に係る自身の編集を修正、および陵墓項の序文表記統一。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=軽里大塚古墳
（前の山古墳）| 所属=[[古市古墳群]]| 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里3丁目| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=軽里大塚古墳（伝日本武尊陵）}}| 形状=[[前方後円墳]]| 築造年代=[[5世紀]]後半| 規模=墳丘長190m
高さ23m| 史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定「白鳥陵」| 被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）[[日本武尊]]| 出土品=[[埴輪]]| 特記事項=全国第36位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2015年10月13日更新版）。| 画像=[[画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun.jpg|none|250px]] }} [[画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{国土航空写真}}]] '''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''白鳥陵'''（しらとりのみささぎ）」として第12代[[景行天皇]]皇子の[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の陵に治定されている。 別称を「'''前の山古墳'''」とも。羽曳野市の名前の由来となった古墳である。 ==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、[[白髪山古墳]]の北東に位置する。全長約190m、後円部径約106m、高さ約20m、前方部幅約165m、高さ約23mで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で前方部を西に向け、くびれ部の両側には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲には幅約35mの周濠と上面幅21mの堤がめぐらされている。 主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の円筒[[埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの形象埴輪が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。 == アクセス == [[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]より西に徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるさとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史‎]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: 「白鳥陵」と埴輪、経由地について
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=軽里大塚古墳
（前の山古墳）| 所属=[[古市古墳群]]| 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里3丁目| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=軽里大塚古墳（伝日本武尊陵）}}| 形状=[[前方後円墳]]| 築造年代=[[5世紀]]後半| 規模=墳丘長190m
高さ23m| 史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定「白鳥陵」| 被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）[[日本武尊]]| 出土品=[[埴輪]]| 特記事項=全国第36位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2015年10月13日更新版）。| 画像=[[画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun.jpg|none|250px]] }} [[画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{国土航空写真}}]] '''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''[[白鳥陵]]'''（しらとりのみささぎ）」として第12代[[景行天皇]]皇子の[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の陵に治定されている。 別称を「'''前の山古墳'''」とも。羽曳野市の名前の由来となった古墳である。 ==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、[[白髪山古墳]]の北東に位置する。全長約190m、後円部径約106m、高さ約20m、前方部幅約165m、高さ約23mで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で前方部を西に向け、くびれ部の両側には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲には幅約35mの周濠と上面幅21mの堤がめぐらされている。 主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の[[円筒埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。 == アクセス == [[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]より[[国道166号|白鳥交差点]]を経て西に徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるさとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

墳丘全景（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） }} '''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''[[白鳥陵]]'''（しらとりのみささぎ）」として第12代[[景行天皇]]皇子の[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の陵に治定されている。 別称を「'''前の山古墳'''」や「'''白鳥陵古墳'''」とも。羽曳野市の名前の由来となった古墳である。 ==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、[[白髪山古墳]]の北東に位置する。全長約190[[メートル|m]]、後円部径約106m、高さ約20m、前方部幅約165m、高さ約23mで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で前方部を西に向け、くびれ部の両側には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲には幅約35mの周濠と上面幅21mの堤がめぐらされている。 主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の[[円筒埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。 画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{国土航空写真}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]白鳥陵 拝所 == アクセス == [[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]より[[国道166号|白鳥交差点]]を経て西に徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるさとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

墳丘全景（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[3DCG]]で描画した軽里大塚古墳}}]] '''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]の[[古市古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''[[白鳥陵]]'''（しらとりのみささぎ）」として第12代[[景行天皇]]皇子の[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の陵に治定されている。 別称を「'''前の山古墳'''」や「'''白鳥陵古墳'''」とも。羽曳野市の名前の由来となった古墳である。 ==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、[[白髪山古墳]]の北東に位置する。全長約190[[メートル|m]]、後円部径約106m、高さ約20m、前方部幅約165m、高さ約23mで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で前方部を西に向け、くびれ部の両側には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲には幅約35mの周濠と上面幅21mの堤がめぐらされている。 主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の[[円筒埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。 2020年度に周堤が国の[[史跡]]に指定される予定である（史跡「古市古墳群」への追加指定）{{Cite web |url= https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/92657301.html|title=文化審議会の答申（史跡等の指定等）について|publisher=文化庁|accessdate=2020-11-25}}。（官報告示を経て正式指定となる） 画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{国土航空写真}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]白鳥陵 拝所 File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。前方部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。後円部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。北西側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画。真横から見る == アクセス == [[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]より[[国道166号|白鳥交差点]]を経て西に徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥古墳]] - [[熱田神宮]]社伝により日本武尊の陵とされる == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるさとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称=軽里大塚古墳 |別名=前の山古墳/白鳥陵古墳 |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里3丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=軽里大塚古墳（伝日本武尊陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |築造年代=[[5世紀]]後半 |規模=墳丘長190[[メートル|m]]

（「[[古市古墳群]]」に包含） |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）[[日本武尊]] |出土品=[[埴輪]] |特記事項=全国第36位の規模
（墳丘長200mとする場合）[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。 |画像=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
墳丘全景（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[3DCG]]で描画した軽里大塚古墳}}]] '''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''[[白鳥陵]]'''（しらとりのみささぎ）」として第12代[[景行天皇]]皇子の[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の陵に治定されている。また周堤は国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち）令和3年3月26日文部科学省告示第48号。。 別称を「'''前の山古墳'''」や「'''白鳥陵古墳'''」とも。羽曳野市の名前の由来となった古墳である。 ==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、[[白髪山古墳]]の北東に位置する。全長約190[[メートル|m]]、後円部径約106m、高さ約20m、前方部幅約165m、高さ約23mで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で前方部を西に向け、くびれ部の両側には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲には幅約35mの周濠と上面幅21mの堤がめぐらされている。 主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の[[円筒埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。 2021年に周堤が国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」への追加指定）。 画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{国土航空写真}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]白鳥陵 拝所 File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。前方部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。後円部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。北西側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画。真横から見る == アクセス == [[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]より[[国道166号|白鳥交差点]]を経て西に徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥古墳]] - [[熱田神宮]]社伝により日本武尊の陵とされる == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるさとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

（「[[古市古墳群]]」に包含） |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）[[日本武尊]] |出土品=[[埴輪]] |特記事項=全国第36位の規模

墳丘全景（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[3DCG]]で描画した軽里大塚古墳}}]] '''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''[[白鳥陵]]'''（しらとりのみささぎ）」として[[日本武尊]]（[[景行天皇]]皇子、[[仲哀天皇]]父）の陵に治定されている。また周堤は国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち）令和3年3月26日文部科学省告示第48号。。 別称を「'''前の山古墳'''」や「'''白鳥陵古墳'''」とも。羽曳野市の名前の由来となった古墳である。 ==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、[[白髪山古墳]]の北東に位置する。全長約190[[メートル|m]]、後円部径約106m、高さ約20m、前方部幅約165m、高さ約23mで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で前方部を西に向け、北側のくびれ部には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲には幅約35mの周濠と上面幅21mの堤がめぐらされている。 主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の[[円筒埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。 2021年に周堤が国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」への追加指定）。 画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{国土航空写真}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]白鳥陵 拝所 File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。前方部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。後円部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。北西側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画。真横から見る == アクセス == [[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]より[[国道166号|白鳥交差点]]を経て西に徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥古墳]] - [[熱田神宮]]社伝により日本武尊の陵とされる == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるさとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

墳丘全景（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[3DCG]]で描画した軽里大塚古墳}}]] '''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 [[宮内庁]]により「'''[[白鳥陵]]'''（しらとりのみささぎ）」として[[日本武尊]]（[[景行天皇]]皇子、[[仲哀天皇]]父）の陵に治定されている。また周堤は国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち）令和3年3月26日文部科学省告示第48号。。 別称を「'''前の山古墳'''」や「'''白鳥稜'''」とも。羽曳野市の名前の由来となった古墳である。 ==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、[[白髪山古墳]]の北東に位置する。全長約190[[メートル|m]]、後円部径約106m、高さ約20m、前方部幅約165m、高さ約23mで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で前方部を西に向け、北側のくびれ部には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲には幅約35mの周濠と上面幅21mの堤がめぐらされている。 主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の[[円筒埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。 2021年に周堤が国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」への追加指定）。 画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{国土航空写真}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]白鳥陵 拝所 File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。前方部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。後円部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。北西側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画。真横から見る == アクセス == [[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]より[[国道166号|白鳥交差点]]を経て西に徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥古墳]] - [[熱田神宮]]社伝により日本武尊の陵とされる == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるさとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: 巻き戻し：陵墓の表記統一、「稜」は誤り。
revision.text:

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Japanese kofun |Name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |Coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th centur..."
revision.text:
{{Japanese kofun |Name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |Location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |Coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |Image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]]

Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]]

A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. (if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]軽里大塚古墳]]

The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] 軽里大塚古墳]]

Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. (if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:test if the jawiki is actually present]] 軽里大塚古墳]]

The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dd]] 軽里大塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |Name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] 軽里大塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |Name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] 軽里大塚古墳]]

File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] 軽里大塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] 軽里大塚古墳]]

File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] 軽里大塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] 軽里大塚古墳]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) refers to various {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} in Japan. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)|ja|大塚古墳 (須賀川市)}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|駒形大塚古墳}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那須郡那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} - Located in {{il..."

* ka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yada Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site.

Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] to [[Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] 軽里大塚古墳]]

Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} -
