revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] 軽里大塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Tumulus length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Tumulus overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} (Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
