revision.comment: Text replacement - "|日本武尊}}" to "|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |Artifacts=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |Construction period=Late 5th century |Size=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun Sizes (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters

Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun |Group={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in shapes such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus shape—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-typed Kofun|Keyhole-typed]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-typed and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |Alias=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National Historic Site, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |other_designation=National other_designation "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National other_designation as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National other_designation, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designated as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |other_designation=National other_designation "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |Notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designated a National other_designation as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designated a National other_designation, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designation1 a National other_designation, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] designation1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |other_designation=National other_designation "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |Burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency designation) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that form the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designation1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been designation1 a National other_designation as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was designation1 a National other_designation, as an addition[[Category:addition]]al part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == *
