revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=draft|12=draft}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=draft|12=draft}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=draft|12=draft}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=draft|12=draft}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]]

Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=draft|12=draft}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=draft|12=draft}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]]

(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=draft}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|12=draft}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=draft}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=draft}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=draft}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|12=draft}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]]

revision.comment: 47 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:軽里大塚古墳 up to rev 94784552
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=軽里大塚古墳 |別名=前の山古墳/白鳥陵古墳 |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里3丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=軽里大塚古墳（伝日本武尊陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |築造年代=[[5世紀]]後半 |規模=墳丘長190[[メートル|m]]
高さ23m |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「白鳥陵」 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「白鳥陵古墳周堤」
（「[[古市古墳群]]」に包含） |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）[[日本武尊]] |出土品=[[埴輪]] |特記事項=全国第36位の規模
（墳丘長200mとする場合）[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。 |画像=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
墳丘全景（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[3DCG]]で描画した軽里大塚古墳}}]] '''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''[[白鳥陵]]'''（しらとりのみささぎ）」として[[日本武尊]]（[[景行天皇]]皇子、[[仲哀天皇]]父）の陵に治定されている。また周堤は国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち）令和3年3月26日文部科学省告示第48号。。 別称を「'''前の山古墳'''」や「'''白鳥陵古墳'''」とも。羽曳野市の名前の由来となった古墳である。 ==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、[[白髪山古墳]]の北東に位置する。全長約190[[メートル|m]]、後円部径約106m、高さ約20m、前方部幅約165m、高さ約23mで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で前方部を西に向け、北側のくびれ部には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲には幅約35mの周濠と上面幅21mの堤がめぐらされている。 主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の[[円筒埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。 2021年に周堤が国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」への追加指定）。 画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{国土航空写真}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]白鳥陵 拝所 File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。前方部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。後円部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。北西側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画。真横から見る == アクセス == [[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]より[[国道166号|白鳥交差点]]を経て西に徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥古墳]] - [[熱田神宮]]社伝により日本武尊の陵とされる == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるさとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

（「[[古市古墳群]]」に包含） |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）[[日本武尊]] |出土品=[[埴輪]] |特記事項=全国第36位の規模
（墳丘長200mとする場合）[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。 |画像=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
墳丘全景（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[3DCG]]で描画した軽里大塚古墳}}]] '''軽里大塚古墳'''（かるさとおおつかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]軽里にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]（[[世界文化遺産]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''[[白鳥陵]]'''（しらとりのみささぎ）」として[[日本武尊]]（[[景行天皇]]皇子、[[仲哀天皇]]父）の陵に治定されている。また周堤は国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち）令和3年3月26日文部科学省告示第48号。。 別称を「'''前の山古墳'''」や「'''白鳥陵古墳'''」とも。羽曳野市の名前の由来となった古墳である。 ==概要== [[古市古墳群]]の南部、[[墓山古墳]]の南、[[白髪山古墳]]の北東に位置する。全長約190[[メートル|m]]、後円部径約106m、高さ約20m、前方部幅約165m、高さ約23mで、古市古墳群で7番目の大きさである。墳丘は三段築成で前方部を西に向け、北側のくびれ部には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲には幅約35mの周濠と上面幅21mの堤がめぐらされている。 主体部や副葬品については不明である。1981年の発掘調査で、後円部の[[円筒埴輪]]列が確認されたほか、朝顔形埴輪や家、蓋などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。これらの出土品や、前方部幅が後円部径に比べて約1.5倍もあるという墳形上の特徴から、古市古墳群では[[市ノ山古墳]]とほぼ同時期、5世紀後半の築造と推定されている。 2021年に周堤が国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」への追加指定）。 画像:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{国土航空写真}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]白鳥陵 拝所 File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。前方部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。後円部側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCGで描画。北西側から見る File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCGで描画。真横から見る == アクセス == [[近鉄南大阪線]][[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]より[[国道166号|白鳥交差点]]を経て西に徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|白鳥古墳]] - [[熱田神宮]]社伝により日本武尊の陵とされる == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かるさとおおつかこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓かるさとおおつかこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=draft|12=draft}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=draft|12=draft}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} |location={{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino, Osaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|qq=}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino, Osaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|qq=}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino, Osaka|qq=}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|qq=}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinoyama Kofun|qq=}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|ja|古市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|qq=}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|qq=}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]]

Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}). Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino, Osaka|qq=}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|qq=}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (prince of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|qq=}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]], also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]], passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|qq=}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]]. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]]

Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|qq=}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|qq=}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (prince of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|qq=}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]], also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]], passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|qq=}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]]. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace redirect category → Kofun of Osaka prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of=[[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] |location=[[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|qq=}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|qq=}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (prince of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|qq=}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]], also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]], passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|qq=}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]]. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|qq=}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]], also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]], passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|qq=}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]]. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of=[[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] |location=[[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Karusato 3-chome |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|4.33|N|135|36|13.78|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun (Designated Tomb of Yamato Takeru)}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} |burial goods=[[Haniwa]] |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=[[File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|none|280px]]
Kofun overview (front part on the right, back part in the left rear) }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|qq=}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|qq=}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (prince of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|qq=}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]], also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]], passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|qq=}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]]. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Karisato Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}}
