Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]], also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]], passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]]. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11637613]] Wikidata name: Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}; {{ill|Shiratori no Misasagi|WD=Q11580607}} |location={{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.5511|N|135.6044|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | Interred_individual = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 3次元コンピュータグラフィックス|1=3D computer graphics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q189177}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} (prince of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]], also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]], passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]]. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | Interred_individual = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 3次元コンピュータグラフィックス|1=3D computer graphics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q189177}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} (prince of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]], also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]], passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]]. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]], passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]]. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11637613]] Wikidata name: Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}; {{ill|Shiratori no Misasagi|WD=Q11580607}} |location={{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.5511|N|135.6044|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]]) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | Interred_individual = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 3次元コンピュータグラフィックス|1=3D computer graphics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q189177}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} (prince of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] and father of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]], also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from [[:en:Furuichi Station|Furuichi Station]] on the [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]], passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]]. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | Interred_individual = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 3次元コンピュータグラフィックス|1=3D computer graphics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q189177}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|en|Furuichi Station|WD=Q1207162|lt=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. (if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | Interred_individual = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 3次元コンピュータグラフィックス|1=3D computer graphics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q189177}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture. Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|en|Furuichi Station|WD=Q1207162|lt=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|en|Furuichi Station|WD=Q1207162|lt=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}; {{ill|Shiratori no Misasagi|WD=Q11580607}} |location={{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.5511|N|135.6044|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | Interred_individual = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 3次元コンピュータグラフィックス|1=3D computer graphics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q189177}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|en|Furuichi Station|WD=Q1207162|lt=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | Interred_individual = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 3次元コンピュータグラフィックス|1=3D computer graphics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q189177}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=Cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|en|Furuichi Station|WD=Q1207162|lt=Furuichi Station|1=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | Interred_individual = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 3次元コンピュータグラフィックス|1=3D computer graphics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q189177}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=Cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|WD=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|en|Furuichi Station|WD=Q1207162|lt=Furuichi Station|1=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | Interred_individual = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 3次元コンピュータグラフィックス|1=3D computer graphics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q189177}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|qid=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}, Osaka Prefecture. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|qid=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|qid=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|en|Furuichi Station|qid=Q1207162|lt=Furuichi Station|1=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|qid=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|qid=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|qid=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|en|Furuichi Station|qid=Q1207162|lt=Furuichi Station|1=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|qid=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|qid=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpōkōenfun]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Karusato Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] |name=Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun |alternate_name=Maenoyama Kofun/Shiratori Ryo Kofun | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}; {{ill|Shiratori no Misasagi|qid=Q11580607|1=Shiratori Ryo}} |location={{ill|Habikino|qid=Q694308|1=Habikino}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.5511|N|135.6044|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |built=Late 5th century |area=Kofun length: 190 meters
Height: 23 meters |Imperial tomb=[[Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site1 as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} |Historic Site=National Historic Site "Shiratori Ryo Kofun Moat"
(Part of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}) |burial figure=(Imperial Household Agency Historic Site) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} |burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |notes=36th largest in Japan
(if tumulus length is considered 200m)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of Kofun areas (National)] (Sakai City Website, Updated May 13, 2018). |image=File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | Interred_individual = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} }} [[File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun visualized in {{ill|3DCG|ja|3DCG|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/3DCG|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 3次元コンピュータグラフィックス|1=3D computer graphics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q189177}}}}]] '''Karusato Ōtsuka Kofun''' (軽里大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|qid=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped and is one of the tumuli that type the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|世界文化遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=Cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q210272}}. Although the actual person interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}}, the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} (prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} and father of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}). Additionally, the surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Group.Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 48, March 26, 2021. Also referred to as "Maenoyama Kofun" or "Shiratori Ryo Kofun," the tumulus is the namesake of {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|qid=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}. == Overview == Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, the tumulus lies south of {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}} and northeast of {{ill|Shiragiyama Kofun|ja|白髪山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiragiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 190 meters in length, with the back circular portion measuring about 106 meters in diameter and 20 meters in height. The front rectangular portion is approximately 165 meters wide and 23 meters high. The tumulus has three tiers, with the front portion oriented westward. A {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Protrusion (kofun)|ja|造り出し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Protrusion (kofun)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} is found in the northern constriction area, and the mound is surrounded by a 35-meter-wide moat and a 21-meter-wide embankment. The primary burial chamber and grave goods are unknown. However, an excavation conducted in 1981 confirmed a {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} row at the back portion of the mound, along with haniwa in types such as morning glory flowers, houses, and lids. Based on these finds and the tumulus type—where the width of the front rectangular portion is approximately 1.5 times the diameter of the back circular portion—the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the late 5th century, around the same time as {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|en|Ichinoyama Kofun|qid=Q11479972|lt=Ichinoyama Kofun|1=Ichinoyama Kofun}}, also part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. In 2021, the surrounding moat was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site, as an additional part of the Furuichi Kofun Group. File:Karusato-Ootsuka-Kofun 02.jpg|{{National Land Aerial Photography Program}} File:Karusato Otsuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area for {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} Shiratori Ryo File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the front portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the rear circular portion File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3DCG rendering, view from the northwest File:CG KS Otsuka Kofun 5 7p.jpg|3DCG rendering, side view == Access == The site is a 10-minute walk west from {{ill|Furuichi Station|en|Furuichi Station|qid=Q1207162|lt=Furuichi Station|1=Furuichi Station}} on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|qid=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}, passing the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on National Route 166. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related articles == * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} - Believed to be the tomb of Yamato Takeru according to the records of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|qid=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}. == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|軽里大塚古墳|version=94784552|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Karusato Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11637613}} [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
